overview.summary: The ING module handles ingestion of reference data and daily updates from multiple external source types, generates derived snapshots, normalizes data against the product canonical, and exposes query/export APIs for downstream consumption. It is the operational core of NMD — everything between source and consumer.
overview.purpose: This document specifies the functional requirements for the Multi-Source Ingestion, Normalization & Query module within the Narwhal Market Data platform. It translates PC-NMD-0001 requirements FR-002 through FR-008 into detailed, testable specifications.
overview.audience: Developers, QA engineers, data owners, and downstream system integrators.

in_scope: Reference data ingestion from external sources (FR-002)
Daily update ingestion — settlement prices, margin rates, corporate events (FR-003)
Multi-source type support — exchange websites, public APIs, SFTP, structured documents (CSV, XML, PDF), data feeds (FR-004)
Derived snapshot generation from multiple external sources (FR-005)
Data normalization and clean-up linked to product canonical (FR-006)
Centralized ingestion framework to manage and track all source processing (FR-007)
Query and export APIs for downstream system consumption (FR-008)
out_of_scope: UI for data owners to manage sources, ingestion, and data (FR-009)
Onboarding new data feeds via common framework without core changes (FR-010)
Real-time streaming and subscription-based data delivery (Phase 3)
Direct integration or data delivery to downstream systems (Phase 2)

items.definition: A point-in-time extract of data from one or more sources, used as input to the normalization pipeline.
A snapshot generated by combining and transforming data from multiple source snapshots, constructed as another source by parsing source data in a configured step sequence.
The centralized component that manages, schedules, tracks, and monitors the processing of all data sources.
A pluggable component that handles connectivity and parsing for a specific source type (e.g., SFTP, REST API, website scraper).
Storage tier containing the latest reference data and latest daily update (date and version) per instrument. This is the data served by the query API.
Storage tier containing all previous versions and previous dates of data. Retained for audit and historical queries.

business_context.content: This module delivers on the core NMD value proposition — eliminating direct vendor/exchange dependencies by centralizing ingestion and normalization. It provides a single, authoritative pipeline from external data sources to normalized, queryable data for downstream consumption.

items.name: Ingestion Framework
items.description: Orchestrates scheduled and on-demand ingestion runs

items.name: External Data Source
items.description: Exchanges, vendors — provides raw data via SFTP/API/website/files

items.name: Product Canonical
items.description: Provides master instrument records for normalization linking (FRD-NMD-0001)

items.name: Downstream System
items.description: Consumes normalized data via query/export APIs

items.name: Data Owner
items.description: Monitors ingestion, triggers manual runs, validates output

functional_overview.content: The ING module operates as a pipeline with four stages: (1) Source Connectivity — adapters connect to external sources (SFTP, API, website, document, feed) and pull raw data; (2) Ingestion — raw data is parsed and stored as source snapshots, with reference data and daily updates handled separately; (3) Normalization — source snapshots are cleaned, transformed, and linked to product canonical records, and derived snapshots are generated from multiple sources; (4) Query/Export — normalized data is exposed via APIs for downstream consumption. The Ingestion Framework (FR-007) manages and tracks all stages.

items.initiates: FR-ING-007, FR-ING-008, FR-ING-010, FR-ING-011, FR-ING-012 — pull from configured sources

items.trigger: Manual ingestion trigger by data owner
items.initiates: FR-ING-007, FR-ING-008, FR-ING-010, FR-ING-011, FR-ING-012 — on-demand pull for specific source

items.trigger: Source file/data available (e.g. SFTP file landed)
items.initiates: FR-ING-007, FR-ING-008, FR-ING-010, FR-ING-011, FR-ING-012 — event-driven ingestion
FR-ING-014, FR-ING-015, FR-ING-016 — triggers normalization and snapshot derivation
FR-ING-024 through FR-ING-028 — API serves normalized data

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from exchange websites via HTTP/HTTPS
requirements.acceptance_criteria: At least one exchange website source adapter successfully ingests data

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from public APIs (REST/JSON)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: At least one REST API source adapter successfully ingests data

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from SFTP file drops
requirements.acceptance_criteria: SFTP adapter connects, detects new files, and ingests successfully

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from structured documents (CSV, XML, PDF)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Document adapter parses CSV, XML, and PDF formats and extracts data correctly

requirements.description: System shall support ingestion from data feeds
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Feed adapter connects and ingests data from at least one feed source

requirements.description: Each source type shall be handled by a pluggable source adapter with a common interface
requirements.acceptance_criteria: New source adapter can be added without modifying core ingestion framework code

requirements.description: System shall ingest product static data (contract specs, tick sizes, lot sizes) from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Product static data ingested and stored as versioned source snapshot

requirements.description: System shall ingest contract listings (expiry dates, series, strike prices) from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Contract listings ingested and stored as versioned source snapshot

requirements.description: System shall store ingested reference data as versioned source snapshots
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Each ingestion run creates a new snapshot version; previous versions are retained in archive layer

requirements.description: System shall ingest daily settlement prices from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Settlement prices ingested with correct date and version tagging

requirements.description: System shall ingest margin rates from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Margin rates ingested and stored as dated source snapshot

requirements.description: System shall ingest corporate events from configured sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Corporate events ingested and stored as dated source snapshot

requirements.description: System shall store ingested daily updates as dated source snapshots
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Each daily update stored with date stamp; multiple intraday updates create new versions with latest tagged

requirements.description: System shall generate derived snapshots by combining data from multiple source snapshots for the same instrument
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Derived snapshot correctly constructed via configured step sequence with later steps overriding earlier values

requirements.description: System shall normalize ingested data to a standard internal format
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Output data conforms to internal schema regardless of source format

requirements.description: System shall link each normalized record to its corresponding product canonical record
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Every normalized record carries a canonical record ID or is flagged as unmatched

requirements.description: System shall flag records that cannot be matched to a canonical record for manual review
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Unmatched records visible with unmatched status; data owner can review and resolve

requirements.description: System shall provide a centralized registry of all configured data sources
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Source registry lists all configured sources with connection details and schedules

requirements.description: System shall support scheduled ingestion runs (configurable per source)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Scheduled runs execute at configured times without manual intervention

requirements.description: System shall support manual on-demand ingestion for a specific source
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Data owner can trigger ingestion for a specific source and monitor its progress

requirements.description: System shall detect source file availability and trigger ingestion automatically
requirements.acceptance_criteria: File landing on SFTP or equivalent triggers ingestion within configured detection interval

requirements.description: System shall track ingestion run status (pending, running, completed, failed) per source
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Run status queryable in real time; historical runs retained

requirements.description: System shall log all ingestion activity with source, timestamp, record counts, and outcome
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Audit log contains source ID, run timestamp, records processed, records failed, and final outcome

requirements.description: System shall expose a query API to retrieve normalized reference data by instrument, product type, or exchange
requirements.acceptance_criteria: API returns correct reference data filtered by requested parameters

requirements.description: System shall expose a query API to retrieve daily settlement prices by instrument and date/date range
requirements.acceptance_criteria: API returns correct settlement prices for requested instrument and date range

requirements.description: System shall expose a query API to retrieve margin rates by instrument and date
requirements.acceptance_criteria: API returns correct margin rates for requested instrument and date

requirements.description: System shall support bulk export of normalized data as file download (CSV/JSON)
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Bulk export generates downloadable file in requested format with correct data

requirements.description: System shall support filtering, pagination, and sorting on all query endpoints
requirements.acceptance_criteria: All query endpoints accept filter, page, page_size, and sort parameters and return correct results

items.rule: A source snapshot must be fully ingested before normalization begins — no partial processing
Derived snapshots are constructed as another source by parsing source data in a configured step sequence — each step overlays the previous, so later steps override earlier values for the same field

Settlement prices may be updated multiple times within the same day (corrections, restatements). Each update creates a new version. The system tracks all versions and tags the latest version as current
Normalization must not create or modify product canonical records — only link to existing ones
Ingestion runs for the same source must not execute concurrently — queue or reject if already running
All API query results return data from the latest completed normalization run only — in-progress data is never exposed

items.system_behavior: Retry up to configured max attempts with backoff; log each attempt
items.user_visible_outcome: Ingestion run marked 'Failed' with error detail; alert raised

items.system_behavior: Abort ingestion for that source; do not process partial data
items.user_visible_outcome: Run marked 'Failed — Schema Mismatch'; data owner notified

items.trigger_condition: Record cannot be matched to a canonical record during normalization
items.system_behavior: Record flagged as 'Unmatched'; normalization continues for remaining records
items.user_visible_outcome: Unmatched records visible in query API with unmatched status; data owner review required

items.trigger_condition: Duplicate ingestion run triggered (same source, same schedule window)
items.user_visible_outcome: Data owner informed run was skipped — previous run still active

items.system_behavior: Log warning; do not overwrite previous snapshot with empty data
items.user_visible_outcome: Run marked 'Completed — Empty'; previous snapshot remains current

apis_provided.name: Query Reference Data
apis_provided.description: Retrieve normalized reference data by instrument, product type, or exchange

apis_provided.name: Query Settlement Prices
apis_provided.description: Retrieve settlement prices by instrument and date/date range

apis_provided.name: Query Margin Rates
apis_provided.description: Retrieve margin rates by instrument and date

apis_provided.name: Ingestion Status
apis_provided.description: Query ingestion run status per source

data_owned.notes: Computed from source snapshots via step sequence
Linked to canonical, serves query API

items.purpose: Linking normalized data to master records (FR-ING-016)

items.what_consumed: Raw market data (SFTP, API, website, files, feeds)

entities.name: Source Snapshot
entities.description: Raw ingested data from external sources, versioned

entities.name: Derived Snapshot
entities.description: Computed from source snapshots via configured step sequence

entities.name: Normalized Record
entities.description: Linked to canonical, serves query API

entities.name: Ingestion Run Log
entities.description: Operational audit trail for all ingestion activity

entities.name: Source Configuration
entities.description: Source registry, connection details, schedules, step sequences

entities.name: Unmatched Record
entities.description: Flagged for data owner review during normalization

data_requirements.data_retention: Two-tier storage model: Active layer contains the latest reference data and latest daily update (date and version) per instrument — this is what the query API serves. Archive layer contains all previous versions and previous dates, retained for audit and historical queries. No specific purge policy defined yet.

items.description: Daily settlement file processing must complete within SLA window (ref NFR-001: T+1 by 08:00)
items.design_impact: Pipeline must be optimized for batch throughput; may require parallel source processing

items.description: Query API responses must meet latency target (ref NFR-004: <500ms p95)
items.design_impact: Normalized data must be pre-indexed/materialized, not computed on query

items.description: All ingested data must be traceable to its source with full audit log (ref NFR-003)
items.design_impact: Every record carries source ID, snapshot version, ingestion run ID

items.description: Ingestion failures must trigger operational alerts (ref NFR-008)
items.design_impact: Framework must integrate with alerting; EXC-001 through EXC-005 all produce alerts

items.description: Source adapter addition must not require changes to core ingestion framework (ref NFR-006)
items.design_impact: Adapter interface must be stable and pluggable

matrix.description: Ingest reference data from external sources
Ingest daily updates from external sources
Generate derived snapshots from multiple sources

items.criteria: At least one source adapter successfully ingests reference data and daily updates from an external source
Derived snapshot generation produces correct output from multiple source inputs in configured step order
All normalized records are linked to a canonical record or flagged as unmatched
Query API returns normalized data with <500ms p95 latency
Ingestion framework tracks run status and logs all activity with full source traceability
Settlement price versioning works correctly — multiple intraday updates tracked, latest tagged
All five exception scenarios (EXC-001 through EXC-005) behave as specified

assumptions.description: Exchange files for Futures and Options and FX are accessible via public or permissioned sources (SFTP, website, API)
Exchange selection for Futures and Options will be confirmed before detailed design begins

constraints.description: Data types limited to Futures and Options and FX in Phase 1
Exchange selection for Futures and Options TBC

items.description: Exchange or official source access credentials or agreements for Futures and Options (TBC)
Confirmation of target exchanges for Phase 1 Futures and Options data
Product Canonical module (FRD-NMD-0001) operational for normalization linking
